page: Vultr vcg-a16-12c-128g-32vram is a Cloud GPU server configured with an Intel x86_64 processor featuring 12 shared vCPUs, derived from 6 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The system operates at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz and is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding 10.67 GB of RAM per core. Graphics and parallel compute acceleration are delivered via two NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPUs, providing a combined total of 32 GB VRAM. Local storage is supported by a 700 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates average-tier performance for general-purpose compute and memory operations, while showing top-tier capabilities in specific language model workloads. The stress-ng multi-core CPU benchmark registered a score of 21080.54, and the Passmark CPU score reached 20085.48. Large-block memory bandwidth tests recorded 102.38 GB/sec for read operations and 53.61 GB/sec for write operations. For LLM inference, the server achieved top-tier prompt processing speeds of 8665.1 tokens/sec with a small 135M model and 613.27 tokens/sec with a medium 7B model, alongside strong text generation speeds of 260.3 tokens/sec and 32.35 tokens/sec respectively.

From a resource density perspective, this server offers a qualitatively cost-efficient configuration for workloads requiring multi-GPU setups without the high cost of dedicated enterprise-grade CPU cores. However, because the CPU allocation is shared, users may experience performance variability during sustained, CPU-heavy multi-core tasks, as indicated by its average-tier CPU benchmark scores.

This server is best suited for parallel processing workloads, including small-to-medium LLM inference, virtual desktop infrastructure (VDI), video transcoding, and memory-intensive database operations that benefit from the 128.0 GB RAM and local NVMe SSD storage.
description: Vultr vcg-a16-12c-128g-32vram is a Cloud GPU server powered by an Intel x86_64 processor with 12 shared vCPUs, 128.0 GB of RAM, and a 700 GB NVMe SSD. It features two NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPUs providing a total of 32 GB VRAM. Benchmarks show average-tier general compute performance, with a Passmark CPU score of 20085.48 and multi-core stress-ng score of 21080.54. However, the server excels in LLM inference, achieving top-tier prompt processing speeds of 8665.1 tokens/sec for a 135M model and 613.27 tokens/sec for a 7B model. The shared CPU architecture presents a potential tradeoff for sustained CPU-bound tasks, but the high memory capacity and dual GPUs make it a qualitatively cost-effective choice for parallel processing. This instance is well-suited for small-to-medium LLM inference, VDI, video transcoding, and memory-intensive database workloads.
og_description: Vultr vcg-a16-12c-128g-32vram is a Cloud GPU server featuring 12 shared Intel vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, a 700 GB NVMe SSD, and two NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPUs with 32 GB total VRAM for parallel workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vcg-a16-12c-128g-32vram is a Cloud GPU server with 12 shared Intel vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and dual NVIDIA A16 GPUs with 32 GB VRAM.
tagline: A dual-GPU accelerated server featuring 32 GB VRAM, 128 GB RAM, and local NVMe storage for parallel processing and inference workloads.
bullet_points: Dual NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPUs with 32 GB total VRAM
12 shared Intel vCPUs and 128.0 GB of system memory
Top-tier prompt processing speeds for 135M and 7B LLM models
Suitable for VDI, video transcoding, and medium-scale model inference
